summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates environment variables via obfuscated postinstall + load-time hand-rolled TLS
details: electron-orbit@1.0.21 executes a multi-stage credential theft chain against installers. The postinstall script (install.js, referenced from package.json scripts.postinstall) enumerates process.env via XOR-obfuscated property lookups (the identifiers 'process' and 'env' are runtime-decoded through an XOR helper __s(key,data)), filters out only keys whose name contains 'path', and writes the remaining environment variables as JSON to os.tmpdir()/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt. On module load, dist/index.js reads that marker file, XORs each byte against the string 'electron-orbit', and hex-encodes the result into a payload variable. The same file implements a full hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client (ClientHello, ECDHE-secp256r1, HKDF, AES-128-GCM, Finished verification) directly over net.connect(port 443), bypassing Node's https module and any TLS interception a defender would rely on; all protocol strings ('node:net', 'connect', HTTP verbs and headers) are XOR-obfuscated. The remote destination is gated behind a kill-switch: it is only populated when sha256(process.env.BuildType) is a substring of a hardcoded hex constant, otherwise the destination is set to the inert literal '%TEMP%', keeping developer laptops and sandboxes dormant while activating in targeted environments. Package metadata claims affiliation with OrbitDB ('OrbitDB Archive', homepage github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron) that is not the real OrbitDB project, borrowing trusted naming to attract installers. Bulk env-var capture at install time collects any secret exported into the installer's shell or CI (AWS_*, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, DB_PASSWORD, etc.).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
